events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need parse scenario. Equipment bonding commercial project. Observed: CSST gas line not bonded. Work rough-complete. Need identify component/condition and visible defect cues. Decision must be fail if dangerous or non-compliant. CSST gas line not bonded is non-compliant per NEC 250.104 (bonding of other metal piping) and 250.30 maybe separately derived systems. Risk high? Gas piping not bonded can be shock/fire hazard, arcing puncture CSST. Critical? In commercial, unbonded CSST can lead to gas leaks/fire/explosion. Likely high or critical. The instruction: dangerous or non-compliant -> fail. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete rough-complete maybe 80? value 0. sound_source etc default empty string/0. workable empty. findings: CSST gas piping lacks required bonding jumper to electrical grounding system; no bonding conductor clamped to CSST fitting/termination; visible absence of bonding jumper at gas line. actions: install listed bonding jumper sized per NEC 250.104 from CSST gas piping to grounded service equipment/grounding electrode conductor; verify continuity; inspect/test. rationale: NEC 250.104 requires metal gas piping to be bonded to service equipment enclosure, etc. CSST is corrugated stainless steel tubing gas piping, susceptible to damage from electrical arcing if not bonded; observed no bonding conductor. references: NEC 250.104, 250.30. Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

  ],

    "Install a listed bonding jumper sized per NEC 250.104 from the CSST gas piping to the grounded service equipment enclosure or grounding electrode conductor",
    "Verify electrical continuity and proper termination of the bonding connection",
    "Re-inspect and test before closing up or energizing"
  ],
  "rationale": "NEC 250.104 requires metal gas piping, including CSST, to be bonded to the service equipment enclosure, grounded conductor, grounding electrode conductor, or grounding electrode. The observed CSST gas line has no bonding jumper, creating a non-compliant, hazardous condition that can lead to arcing, puncture, and fire.",

    "NEC 250.104",

  ]
